NKJV.1: My son, do not forget my law,  But let your heart keep my commands;
NKJV.2: For length of days and long life And peace they will add to you.
NKJV.3: Let not mercy and truth forsake you;  Bind them around your neck,  Write them on the tablet of your heart,
NKJV.4: And so find favor and high esteem In the sight of God and man.
NKJV.5: Trust in the LORD with all your heart,  And lean not on your own understanding;
NKJV.6: In all your ways acknowledge Him, And He shall direct your paths.
NKJV.7: Do not be wise in your own eyes; Fear the LORD and depart from evil.
NKJV.8: It will be health to your flesh, And strength to your bones.
NKJV.9: Honor the LORD with your possessions, And with the firstfruits of all your increase;
NKJV.10: So your barns will be filled with plenty, And your vats will overflow with new wine.
NKJV.11: My son, do not despise the chastening of the LORD, Nor detest His correction;
NKJV.12: For whom the LORD loves He corrects, Just as a father the son in whom he delights.
NKJV.13: Happy is the man who finds wisdom, And the man who gains understanding;
NKJV.14: For her proceeds are better than the profits of silver, And her gain than fine gold.
NKJV.15: She is more precious than rubies, And all the things you may desire cannot compare with her.
NKJV.16: Length of days is in her right hand, In her left hand riches and honor.
NKJV.17: Her ways are ways of pleasantness, And all her paths are peace.
NKJV.18: She is a tree of life to those who take hold of her, And happy are all who retain her.
NKJV.19: The LORD by wisdom founded the earth; By understanding He established the heavens;
NKJV.20: By His knowledge the depths were broken up, And clouds drop down the dew.
NKJV.21: My son, let them not depart from your eyes— Keep sound wisdom and discretion;
NKJV.22: So they will be life to your soul And grace to your neck.
NKJV.23: Then you will walk safely in your way, And your foot will not stumble.
NKJV.24: When you lie down, you will not be afraid; Yes, you will lie down and your sleep will be sweet.
NKJV.25: Do not be afraid of sudden terror, Nor of trouble from the wicked when it comes;
NKJV.26: For the LORD will be your confidence, And will keep your foot from being caught.
NKJV.27: Do not withhold good from those to whom it is due, When it is in the power of your hand to do so.
NKJV.28: Do not say to your neighbor, “Go, and come back, And tomorrow I will give it,” When you have it with you.
NKJV.29: Do not devise evil against your neighbor, For he dwells by you for safety’s sake.
NKJV.30: Do not strive with a man without cause, If he has done you no harm.
NKJV.31: Do not envy the oppressor, And choose none of his ways;
NKJV.32: For the perverse person is an abomination to the LORD, But His secret counsel is with the upright.
NKJV.33: The curse of the LORD is on the house of the wicked, But He blesses the home of the just.
NKJV.34: Surely He scorns the scornful, But gives grace to the humble.
NKJV.35: The wise shall inherit glory, But shame shall be the legacy of fools.

ESV.1: My son, do not forget my teaching, but let your heart keep my commandments,
ESV.2: for length of days and years of life and peace they will add to you.
ESV.3: Let not steadfast love and faithfulness forsake you; bind them around your neck; write them on the tablet of your heart.
ESV.4: So you will find favor and good success  in the sight of God and man.
ESV.5: Trust in the LORD with all your heart, and do not lean on your own understanding.
ESV.6: In all your ways acknowledge him, and he will make straight your paths.
ESV.7: Be not wise in your own eyes; fear the LORD, and turn away from evil.
ESV.8: It will be healing to your flesh  and refreshment  to your bones.
ESV.9: Honor the LORD with your wealth and with the firstfruits of all your produce;
ESV.10: then your barns will be filled with plenty, and your vats will be bursting with wine.
ESV.11: My son, do not despise the LORD's discipline or be weary of his reproof,
ESV.12: for the LORD reproves him whom he loves, as a father the son in whom he delights.
ESV.13: Blessed is the one who finds wisdom, and the one who gets understanding,
ESV.14: for the gain from her is better than gain from silver and her profit better than gold.
ESV.15: She is more precious than jewels, and nothing you desire can compare with her.
ESV.16: Long life is in her right hand; in her left hand are riches and honor.
ESV.17: Her ways are ways of pleasantness, and all her paths are peace.
ESV.18: She is a tree of life to those who lay hold of her; those who hold her fast are called blessed.
ESV.19: The LORD by wisdom founded the earth; by understanding he established the heavens;
ESV.20: by his knowledge the deeps broke open, and the clouds drop down the dew.
ESV.21: My son, do not lose sight of these — keep sound wisdom and discretion,
ESV.22: and they will be life for your soul and adornment for your neck.
ESV.23: Then you will walk on your way securely, and your foot will not stumble.
ESV.24: If you lie down, you will not be afraid; when you lie down, your sleep will be sweet.
ESV.25: Do not be afraid of sudden terror or of the ruin  of the wicked, when it comes,
ESV.26: for the LORD will be your confidence and will keep your foot from being caught.
ESV.27: Do not withhold good from those to whom it is due,  when it is in your power to do it.
ESV.28: Do not say to your neighbor, “Go, and come again, tomorrow I will give it” — when you have it with you.
ESV.29: Do not plan evil against your neighbor, who dwells trustingly beside you.
ESV.30: Do not contend with a man for no reason, when he has done you no harm.
ESV.31: Do not envy a man of violence and do not choose any of his ways,
ESV.32: for the devious person is an abomination to the LORD, but the upright are in his confidence.
ESV.33: The LORD's curse is on the house of the wicked, but he blesses the dwelling of the righteous.
ESV.34: Toward the scorners he is scornful, but to the humble he gives favor.
ESV.35: The wise will inherit honor, but fools get  disgrace.

NASB.1: My son, do not forget my teaching, But let your heart keep my commandments;
NASB.2: For length of days and years of life And peace they will add to you.
NASB.3: Do not let kindness and truth leave you; Bind them around your neck, Write them on the tablet of your heart.
NASB.4: So you will find favor and good repute In the sight of God and man.
NASB.5: Trust in the LORD with all your heart And do not lean on your own understanding.
NASB.6: In all your ways acknowledge Him, And He will make your paths straight.
NASB.7: Do not be wise in your own eyes; Fear the LORD and turn away from evil.
NASB.8: It will be healing to your body And refreshment to your bones.
NASB.9: Honor the LORD from your wealth And from the first of all your produce;
NASB.10: So your barns will be filled with plenty And your vats will overflow with new wine.
NASB.11: My son, do not reject the discipline of the LORD Or loathe His reproof,
NASB.12: For whom the LORD loves He reproves, Even as a father [corrects] the son in whom he delights.
NASB.13: How blessed is the man who finds wisdom And the man who gains understanding.
NASB.14: For her profit is better than the profit of silver And her gain better than fine gold.
NASB.15: She is more precious than jewels; And nothing you desire compares with her.
NASB.16: Long life is in her right hand; In her left hand are riches and honor.
NASB.17: Her ways are pleasant ways And all her paths are peace.
NASB.18: She is a tree of life to those who take hold of her, And happy are all who hold her fast.
NASB.19: The LORD by wisdom founded the earth, By understanding He established the heavens.
NASB.20: By His knowledge the deeps were broken up And the skies drip with dew.
NASB.21: My son, let them not vanish from your sight; Keep sound wisdom and discretion,
NASB.22: So they will be life to your soul And adornment to your neck.
NASB.23: Then you will walk in your way securely And your foot will not stumble.
NASB.24: When you lie down, you will not be afraid; When you lie down, your sleep will be sweet.
NASB.25: Do not be afraid of sudden fear Nor of the onslaught of the wicked when it comes;
NASB.26: For the LORD will be your confidence And will keep your foot from being caught.
NASB.27: Do not withhold good from those to whom it is due, When it is in your power to do [it].
NASB.28: Do not say to your neighbor, 'Go, and come back, And tomorrow I will give [it],' When you have it with you.
NASB.29: Do not devise harm against your neighbor, While he lives securely beside you.
NASB.30: Do not contend with a man without cause, If he has done you no harm.
NASB.31: Do not envy a man of violence And do not choose any of his ways.
NASB.32: For the devious are an abomination to the LORD; But He is intimate with the upright.
NASB.33: The curse of the LORD is on the house of the wicked, But He blesses the dwelling of the righteous.
NASB.34: Though He scoffs at the scoffers, Yet He gives grace to the afflicted.
NASB.35: The wise will inherit honor, But fools display dishonor.

NLT.1:
My child, never forget the things I have taught you.
Store my commands in your heart.
NLT.2:
If you do this, you will live many years,
and your life will be satisfying.
NLT.3:
Never let loyalty and kindness leave you!
Tie them around your neck as a reminder.
Write them deep within your heart.
NLT.4:
Then you will find favor with both God and people,
and you will earn a good reputation.
NLT.5:
Trust in the LORD with all your heart;
do not depend on your own understanding.
NLT.6:
Seek his will in all you do,
and he will show you which path to take.
NLT.7:
Don’t be impressed with your own wisdom.
Instead, fear the LORD and turn away from evil.
NLT.8:
Then you will have healing for your body
and strength for your bones.
NLT.9:
Honor the LORD with your wealth
and with the best part of everything you produce.
NLT.10:
Then he will fill your barns with grain,
and your vats will overflow with good wine.
NLT.11:
My child, don’t reject the LORD’s discipline,
and don’t be upset when he corrects you.
NLT.12:
For the LORD corrects those he loves,
just as a father corrects a child in whom he delights.
NLT.13:
Joyful is the person who finds wisdom,
the one who gains understanding.
NLT.14:
For wisdom is more profitable than silver,
and her wages are better than gold.
NLT.15:
Wisdom is more precious than rubies;
nothing you desire can compare with her.
NLT.16:
She offers you long life in her right hand,
and riches and honor in her left.
NLT.17:
She will guide you down delightful paths;
all her ways are satisfying.
NLT.18:
Wisdom is a tree of life to those who embrace her;
happy are those who hold her tightly.
NLT.19:
By wisdom the LORD founded the earth;
by understanding he created the heavens.
NLT.20:
By his knowledge the deep fountains of the earth burst forth,
and the dew settles beneath the night sky.
NLT.21:
My child, don’t lose sight of common sense and discernment.
Hang on to them,
NLT.22:
for they will refresh your soul.
They are like jewels on a necklace.
NLT.23:
They keep you safe on your way,
and your feet will not stumble.
NLT.24:
You can go to bed without fear;
you will lie down and sleep soundly.
NLT.25:
You need not be afraid of sudden disaster
or the destruction that comes upon the wicked,
NLT.26:
for the LORD is your security.
He will keep your foot from being caught in a trap.
NLT.27:
Do not withhold good from those who deserve it
when it’s in your power to help them.
NLT.28:
If you can help your neighbor now, don’t say,
“Come back tomorrow, and then I’ll help you.”
NLT.29:
Don’t plot harm against your neighbor,
for those who live nearby trust you.
NLT.30:
Don’t pick a fight without reason,
when no one has done you harm.
NLT.31:
Don’t envy violent people
or copy their ways.
NLT.32:
Such wicked people are detestable to the LORD,
but he offers his friendship to the godly.
NLT.33:
The LORD curses the house of the wicked,
but he blesses the home of the upright.
NLT.34:
The LORD mocks the mockers
but is gracious to the humble.
NLT.35:
The wise inherit honor,
but fools are put to shame!

WEB.1: My son, don’t forget my teaching; but let your heart keep my commandments:
WEB.2: for length of days, and years of life, and peace, will they add to you.
WEB.3: Don’t let kindness and truth forsake you. Bind them around your neck. Write them on the tablet of your heart.
WEB.4: So you will find favor, and good understanding in the sight of God and man.
WEB.5: Trust in Yahweh with all your heart, and don’t lean on your own understanding.
WEB.6: In all your ways acknowledge him, and he will make your paths straight.
WEB.7: Don’t be wise in your own eyes. Fear Yahweh, and depart from evil.
WEB.8: It will be health to your body, and nourishment to your bones.
WEB.9: Honor Yahweh with your substance, with the first fruits of all your increase:
WEB.10: so your barns will be filled with plenty, and your vats will overflow with new wine.
WEB.11: My son, don’t despise Yahweh’s discipline, neither be weary of his reproof:
WEB.12: for whom Yahweh loves, he reproves; even as a father reproves the son in whom he delights.
WEB.13: Happy is the man who finds wisdom, the man who gets understanding.
WEB.14: For her good profit is better than getting silver, and her return is better than fine gold.
WEB.15: She is more precious than rubies. None of the things you can desire are to be compared to her.
WEB.16: Length of days is in her right hand. In her left hand are riches and honor.
WEB.17: Her ways are ways of pleasantness. All her paths are peace.
WEB.18: She is a tree of life to those who lay hold of her. Happy is everyone who retains her.
WEB.19: By wisdom Yahweh founded the earth. By understanding, he established the heavens.
WEB.20: By his knowledge, the depths were broken up, and the skies drop down the dew.
WEB.21: My son, let them not depart from your eyes. Keep sound wisdom and discretion:
WEB.22: so they will be life to your soul, and grace for your neck.
WEB.23: Then you shall walk in your way securely. Your foot won’t stumble.
WEB.24: When you lie down, you will not be afraid. Yes, you will lie down, and your sleep will be sweet.
WEB.25: Don’t be afraid of sudden fear, neither of the desolation of the wicked, when it comes:
WEB.26: for Yahweh will be your confidence, and will keep your foot from being taken.
WEB.27: Don’t withhold good from those to whom it is due, when it is in the power of your hand to do it.
WEB.28: Don’t say to your neighbor, “Go, and come again; tomorrow I will give it to you,” when you have it by you.
WEB.29: Don’t devise evil against your neighbor, since he dwells securely by you.
WEB.30: Don’t strive with a man without cause, if he has done you no harm.
WEB.31: Don’t envy the man of violence. Choose none of his ways.
WEB.32: For the perverse is an abomination to Yahweh, but his friendship is with the upright.
WEB.33: Yahweh’s curse is in the house of the wicked, but he blesses the habitation of the righteous.
WEB.34: Surely he mocks the mockers, but he gives grace to the humble.
WEB.35: The wise will inherit glory, but shame will be the promotion of fools.

don’t withhold good ⓒ from the one to whom it belongs.
CSB17.28:
Don’t say to your neighbor, “Go away! Come back later.
I’ll give it tomorrow” — when it is there with you. ⓓ
CSB17.29:
Don’t plan any harm against your neighbor, ⓔ
for he trusts you and lives near you.
CSB17.30:
Don’t accuse anyone without cause, ⓕ
when he has done you no harm.
CSB17.31:
Don’t envy a violent man ⓖ
or choose any of his ways;
CSB17.32:
for the devious are detestable to the LORD,
but he is a friend [9] to the upright. ⓗ
CSB17.33:
The LORD’s curse is on the household of the wicked, ⓘ
but he blesses the home of the righteous;
CSB17.34:
He mocks those who mock,
but gives grace to the humble. ⓙ
CSB17.35:
The wise will inherit honor,
but he holds up fools to dishonor. [10]

AMP.1:
My son, do not forget my teaching,
But let your heart keep my commandments;
AMP.2:
For length of days and years of life [worth living]
And tranquility and prosperity [the wholeness of life's blessings] they will add to you.
AMP.3:
Do not let mercy and kindness and truth leave you [instead let these qualities define you];
Bind them [securely] around your neck,
Write them on the tablet of your heart.
AMP.4:
So find favor and high esteem
In the sight of God and man.
AMP.5:
Trust in and rely confidently on the LORD with all your heart
And do not rely on your own insight or understanding.
AMP.6:
In all your ways know and acknowledge and recognize Him,
And He will make your paths straight and smooth [removing obstacles that block your way].
AMP.7:
Do not be wise in your own eyes;
Fear the LORD [with reverent awe and obedience] and turn [entirely] away from evil.
AMP.8:
It will be health to your body [your marrow, your nerves, your sinews, your muscles—all your inner parts]
And refreshment (physical well-being) to your bones.
AMP.9:
Honor the LORD with your wealth
And with the first fruits of all your crops (income);
AMP.10:
Then your barns will be abundantly filled
And your vats will overflow with new wine.
AMP.11:
My son, do not reject or take lightly the discipline of the LORD [learn from your mistakes and the testing that comes from His correction through discipline];
Nor despise His rebuke,
AMP.12:
For those whom the LORD loves He corrects,
Even as a father corrects the son in whom he delights.
AMP.13:
Happy [blessed, considered fortunate, to be admired] is the man who finds [skillful and godly] wisdom,
And the man who gains understanding and insight [learning from God's word and life's experiences],
AMP.14:
For wisdom's profit is better than the profit of silver,
And her gain is better than fine gold.
AMP.15:
She is more precious than rubies;
And nothing you can wish for compares with her [in value].
AMP.16:
Long life is in her right hand;
In her left hand are riches and honor.
AMP.17:
Her ways are highways of pleasantness and favor,
And all her paths are peace.
AMP.18:
She is a tree of life to those who take hold of her,
And happy [blessed, considered fortunate, to be admired] is everyone who holds her tightly.
AMP.19:
The LORD by His wisdom has founded the earth;
By His understanding He has established the heavens.
AMP.20:
By His knowledge the deeps were broken up
And the clouds drip with dew.
AMP.21:
My son, let them not escape from your sight,
But keep sound wisdom and discretion,
AMP.22:
And they will be life to your soul (your inner self)
And a gracious adornment to your neck (your outer self).
AMP.23:
Then you will walk on your way [of life] securely
And your foot will not stumble.
AMP.24:
When you lie down, you will not be afraid;
When you lie down, your sleep will be sweet.
AMP.25:
Do not be afraid of sudden fear
Nor of the storm of the wicked when it comes [since you will be blameless];
AMP.26:
For the LORD will be your confidence, firm and strong,
And will keep your foot from being caught [in a trap].
AMP.27:
Do not withhold good from those to whom it is due [its rightful recipients],
When it is in your power to do it.
AMP.28:
Do not say to your neighbor, "Go, and come back,
And tomorrow I will give it,"
When you have it with you.
AMP.29:
Do not devise evil against your neighbor,
Who lives securely beside you.
AMP.30:
Do not quarrel with a man without cause,
If he has done you no harm.
AMP.31:
Do not envy a man of violence
And do not choose any of his ways.
AMP.32:
For the devious are repulsive to the LORD;
But His private counsel is with the upright [those with spiritual integrity and moral courage].
AMP.33:
The curse of the LORD is on the house of the wicked,
But He blesses the home of the just and righteous.
AMP.34:
Though He scoffs at the scoffers and scorns the scorners,
Yet He gives His grace [His undeserved favor] to the humble [those who give up self-importance].
AMP.35:
The wise will inherit honor and glory,
But dishonor and shame is conferred on fools.

MSG.1:
Good friend, don't forget all I've taught you;
take to heart my commands.
MSG.2:
They'll help you live a long, long time,
a long life lived full and well.
MSG.3:
Don't lose your grip on Love and Loyalty.
Tie them around your neck; carve their initials on your heart.
MSG.4:
Earn a reputation for living well
in God's eyes and the eyes of the people.
MSG.5:
Trust God from the bottom of your heart;
don't try to figure out everything on your own.
MSG.6:
Listen for God's voice in everything you do, everywhere you go;
he's the one who will keep you on track.
MSG.7:
Don't assume that you know it all.
Run to God! Run from evil!
MSG.8:
Your body will glow with health,
your very bones will vibrate with life!
MSG.9:
Honor God with everything you own;
give him the first and the best.
MSG.10:
Your barns will burst,
your wine vats will brim over.
MSG.11:
But don't, dear friend, resent God's discipline;
don't sulk under his loving correction.
MSG.12:
It's the child he loves that God corrects;
a father's delight is behind all this.
MSG.13:
You're blessed when you meet Lady Wisdom,
when you make friends with Madame Insight.
MSG.14:
She's worth far more than money in the bank;
her friendship is better than a big salary.
MSG.15:
Her value exceeds all the trappings of wealth;
nothing you could wish for holds a candle to her.
MSG.16:
With one hand she gives long life,
with the other she confers recognition.
MSG.17:
Her manner is beautiful,
her life wonderfully complete.
MSG.18:
She's the very Tree of Life to those who embrace her.
Hold her tight—and be blessed!
MSG.19:
With Lady Wisdom, God formed Earth;
with Madame Insight, he raised Heaven.
MSG.20:
They knew when to signal rivers and springs to the surface,
and dew to descend from the night skies.
MSG.21:
Dear friend, guard Clear Thinking and Common Sense with your life;
don't for a minute lose sight of them.
MSG.22:
They'll keep your soul alive and well,
they'll keep you fit and attractive.
MSG.23:
You'll travel safely,
you'll neither tire nor trip.
MSG.24:
You'll take afternoon naps without a worry,
you'll enjoy a good night's sleep.
MSG.25:
No need to panic over alarms or surprises,
or predictions that doomsday's just around the corner,
MSG.26:
Because God will be right there with you;
he'll keep you safe and sound.
MSG.27:
Never walk away from someone who deserves help;
your hand is God's hand for that person.
MSG.28:
Don't tell your neighbor, "Maybe some other time,"
or, "Try me tomorrow,"
when the money's right there in your pocket.
MSG.29:
Don't figure ways of taking advantage of your neighbor
when he's sitting there trusting and unsuspecting.
MSG.30:
Don't walk around with a chip on your shoulder,
always spoiling for a fight.
MSG.31:
Don't try to be like those who shoulder their way through life.
Why be a bully?
MSG.32:
"Why not?" you say. Because God can't stand twisted souls.
It's the straightforward who get his respect.
MSG.33:
God's curse blights the house of the wicked,
but he blesses the home of the righteous.
MSG.34:
He gives proud skeptics a cold shoulder,
but if you're down on your luck, he's right there to help.
MSG.35:
Wise living gets rewarded with honor;
stupid living gets the booby prize.

NIV.1:
Further Benefits of Wisdom
My son, do not forget my teaching,
but keep my commands in your heart,
NIV.2:
for they will prolong your life many years
and bring you prosperity.
NIV.3:
Let love and faithfulness never leave you;
bind them around your neck,
write them on the tablet of your heart.
NIV.4:
Then you will win favor and a good name
in the sight of God and man.
NIV.5:
Trust in the Lord with all your heart
and lean not on your own understanding;
NIV.6:
in all your ways acknowledge him,
and he will make your paths straight.
NIV.7:
Do not be wise in your own eyes;
fear the Lord and shun evil.
NIV.8:
This will bring health to your body
and nourishment to your bones.
NIV.9:
Honor the Lord with your wealth,
with the firstfruits of all your crops;
NIV.10:
then your barns will be filled to overflowing,
and your vats will brim over with new wine.
NIV.11:
My son, do not despise the Lord’s discipline
and do not resent his rebuke,
NIV.12:
because the Lord disciplines those he loves,
as a father the son he delights in.
NIV.13:
Blessed is the man who finds wisdom,
the man who gains understanding,
NIV.14:
for she is more profitable than silver
and yields better returns than gold.
NIV.15:
She is more precious than rubies;
nothing you desire can compare with her.
NIV.16:
Long life is in her right hand;
in her left hand are riches and honor.
NIV.17:
Her ways are pleasant ways,
and all her paths are peace.
NIV.18:
She is a tree of life to those who embrace her;
those who lay hold of her will be blessed.
NIV.19:
By wisdom the Lord laid the earth’s foundations,
by understanding he set the heavens in place;
NIV.20:
by his knowledge the deeps were divided,
and the clouds let drop the dew.
NIV.21:
My son, preserve sound judgment and discernment,
do not let them out of your sight;
NIV.22:
they will be life for you,
an ornament to grace your neck.
NIV.23:
Then you will go on your way in safety,
and your foot will not stumble;
NIV.24:
when you lie down, you will not be afraid;
when you lie down, your sleep will be sweet.
NIV.25:
Have no fear of sudden disaster
or of the ruin that overtakes the wicked,
NIV.26:
for the Lord will be your confidence
and will keep your foot from being snared.
NIV.27:
Do not withhold good from those who deserve it,
when it is in your power to act.
NIV.28:
Do not say to your neighbor,
“Come back later; I’ll give it tomorrow”—
when you now have it with you.
NIV.29:
Do not plot harm against your neighbor,
who lives trustfully near you.
NIV.30:
Do not accuse a man for no reason—
when he has done you no harm.
NIV.31:
Do not envy a violent man
or choose any of his ways,
NIV.32:
for the Lord detests a perverse man
but takes the upright into his confidence.
NIV.33:
The Lord’s curse is on the house of the wicked,
but he blesses the home of the righteous.
NIV.34:
He mocks proud mockers
but gives grace to the humble.
NIV.35:
The wise inherit honor,
but fools he holds up to shame.

NRSVCE.1:
My child, do not forget my teaching,
but let your heart keep my commandments;
NRSVCE.2:
for length of days and years of life
and abundant welfare they will give you.
NRSVCE.3:
Do not let loyalty and faithfulness forsake you;
bind them around your neck,
write them on the tablet of your heart.
NRSVCE.4:
So you will find favor and good repute
in the sight of God and of people.
NRSVCE.5:
Trust in the LORD with all your heart,
and do not rely on your own insight.
NRSVCE.6:
In all your ways acknowledge him,
and he will make straight your paths.
NRSVCE.7:
Do not be wise in your own eyes;
fear the LORD, and turn away from evil.
NRSVCE.8:
It will be a healing for your flesh
and a refreshment for your body.
NRSVCE.9:
Honor the LORD with your substance
and with the first fruits of all your produce;
NRSVCE.10:
then your barns will be filled with plenty,
and your vats will be bursting with wine.
NRSVCE.11:
My child, do not despise the LORD’s discipline
or be weary of his reproof,
NRSVCE.12:
for the LORD reproves the one he loves,
as a father the son in whom he delights.
NRSVCE.13:
Happy are those who find wisdom,
and those who get understanding,
NRSVCE.14:
for her income is better than silver,
and her revenue better than gold.
NRSVCE.15:
She is more precious than jewels,
and nothing you desire can compare with her.
NRSVCE.16:
Long life is in her right hand;
in her left hand are riches and honor.
NRSVCE.17:
Her ways are ways of pleasantness,
and all her paths are peace.
NRSVCE.18:
She is a tree of life to those who lay hold of her;
those who hold her fast are called happy.
NRSVCE.19:
The LORD by wisdom founded the earth;
by understanding he established the heavens;
NRSVCE.20:
by his knowledge the deeps broke open,
and the clouds drop down the dew.
NRSVCE.21:
My child, do not let these escape from your sight:
keep sound wisdom and prudence,
NRSVCE.22:
and they will be life for your soul
and adornment for your neck.
NRSVCE.23:
Then you will walk on your way securely
and your foot will not stumble.
NRSVCE.24:
If you sit down, you will not be afraid;
when you lie down, your sleep will be sweet.
NRSVCE.25:
Do not be afraid of sudden panic,
or of the storm that strikes the wicked;
NRSVCE.26:
for the LORD will be your confidence
and will keep your foot from being caught.
NRSVCE.27:
Do not withhold good from those to whom it is due,
when it is in your power to do it.
NRSVCE.28:
Do not say to your neighbor, “Go, and come again,
tomorrow I will give it” — when you have it with you.
NRSVCE.29:
Do not plan harm against your neighbor
who lives trustingly beside you.
NRSVCE.30:
Do not quarrel with anyone without cause,
when no harm has been done to you.
NRSVCE.31:
Do not envy the violent
and do not choose any of their ways;
NRSVCE.32:
for the perverse are an abomination to the LORD,
but the upright are in his confidence.
NRSVCE.33:
The LORD’s curse is on the house of the wicked,
but he blesses the abode of the righteous.
NRSVCE.34:
Toward the scorners he is scornful,
but to the humble he shows favor.
NRSVCE.35:
The wise will inherit honor,
but stubborn fools, disgrace.

NET.1: My child, do not forget my teaching, but let your heart keep my commandments,
NET.2: for they will provide a long and full life, and they will add well-being to you.
NET.3: Do not let truth and mercy leave you; bind them around your neck, write them on the tablet of your heart.
NET.4: Then you will find favor and good understanding, in the sight of God and people.
NET.5: Trust in the LORD with all your heart, and do not rely on your own understanding.
NET.6: Acknowledge him in all your ways, and he will make your paths straight.
NET.7: Do not be wise in your own estimation; fear the LORD and turn away from evil.
NET.8: This will bring healing to your body, and refreshment to your inner self.
NET.9: Honor the LORD from your wealth and from the first fruits of all your crops;
NET.10: then your barns will be filled completely, and your vats will overflow with new wine.
NET.11: My child, do not despise discipline from the LORD, and do not loathe his rebuke.
NET.12: For the LORD disciplines those he loves, just as a father disciplines the son in whom he delights.
NET.13: Blessed is the one who finds wisdom, and the one who obtains understanding.
NET.14: For her benefit is more profitable than silver, and her gain is better than gold.
NET.15: She is more precious than rubies, and none of the things you desire can compare with her.
NET.16: Long life is in her right hand; in her left hand are riches and honor.
NET.17: Her ways are very pleasant, and all her paths are peaceful.
NET.18: She is like a tree of life to those who obtain her, and everyone who grasps hold of her will be blessed.
NET.19: By wisdom the LORD laid the foundation of the earth; he established the heavens by understanding.
NET.20: By his knowledge the primordial sea was broken open, and the clouds drip down dew.
NET.21: My child, do not let them escape from your sight; safeguard sound wisdom and discretion.
NET.22: So they will give life to you, and grace to adorn your neck.
NET.23: Then you will walk on your way with security, and you will not stumble.
NET.24: When you lie down you will not be filled with fear; when you lie down your sleep will be pleasant.
NET.25: You will not be afraid of sudden disaster, or when destruction overtakes the wicked;
NET.26: for the LORD will be the source of your confidence, and he will guard your foot from being caught in a trap.
NET.27: Do not withhold good from those who need it, when you have the ability to help.
NET.28: Do not say to your neighbor, “Go! Return tomorrow and I will give it,” when you have it with you at the time.
NET.29: Do not plot evil against your neighbor when he dwells by you unsuspectingly.
NET.30: Do not accuse anyone without legitimate cause, if he has not treated you wrongly.
NET.31: Do not envy a violent man, and do not choose to imitate any of his ways;
NET.32: for one who goes astray is an abomination to the LORD, but he reveals his intimate counsel to the upright.
NET.33: The LORD’s curse is on the household of the wicked, but he blesses the home of the righteous.
NET.34: Although he is scornful to arrogant scoffers, yet he shows favor to the humble.
NET.35: The wise inherit honor, but he holds fools up to public contempt.
